news.headline: Analysts reduced forecasts for Accenture after Q2 results. Earnings per share were $2.82 and sales were $16.66 billion.
news.summary: Accenture Plc (NYSE:ACN) announced positive second-quarter results, surpassing expectations. The company reported earnings of $2.82 per share for the quarter ending in 2025, slightly higher than the anticipated $2.81. Their sales for the period reached $16.66 billion, nearly matching the consensus of $16.62 billion and the management's forecast range of $16.2 billion to $16.8 billion. The company highlighted strong client trust and confidence, with 32 clients showing substantial quarterly bookings.

news.headline: SEC dropped XRP case, ending 4-year litigation against Ripple for alleged $1.3B unregistered securities offering in 2020. XRP token unable to stay above $2.5 after 11% rally, fell 6.3%. Market already expected SEC outcome. XRP may rally 75% after lawsuit's end.
news.summary: The United States Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has dropped its legal action against Ripple, ending a four-year litigation against the blockchain developer for an alleged $1.3 billion unregistered securities offering in 2020. Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse revealed this victory on March 19. Analysts believe the market had already priced in this event since President Trump's election, resulting in only an 11% rally for XRP token post-announcement. Despite some technical chart patterns indicating a potential 75% rally for XRP post-lawsuit, the token is struggling to stay above the $2.5 mark and has fallen over 6.3% since the announcement. The market sentiment and overall macro environment are not favorable for XRP, although the SEC's decision will have a positive impact on the market in the long term due to changing narratives and expectations of a more crypto-friendly SEC.

news.headline: Global smart biomaterials market is projected to reach $50.5 billion by 2034, growing at a CAGR of 12.3% from 2024. Key drivers include investments in drug delivery systems and regenerative medicine. Industry leaders are focusing on R&D to enhance applications in tissue engineering and medical devices.
news.summary: The global smart biomaterials market is experiencing rapid growth, with a value of US$14.5 billion in 2023 and projected to reach US$50.5 billion by 2034, at a CAGR of 12.3%. Key factors driving this expansion include investments in drug delivery systems, advancements in regenerative medicine, and the rise in chronic diseases. Industry leaders like Bonalive Biomaterials Ltd., Evonik Industries AG, and Medtronic are focusing on R&D to develop 3D printable biomaterials for tissue engineering, wound healing, and medical devices. Smart biomaterials offer dynamic functionality in applications like drug delivery, tissue engineering, implants, and wound healing, transforming healthcare by improving patient outcomes and reducing complications. Future trends include self-healing biomaterials, AI-powered material informatics, bioelectronics integration, and green chemistry-based biomaterials for eco-conscious medical applications. The market is witnessing a surge in investment in drug delivery systems to meet the demand for controlled drug release and encapsulation solutions. Personalized regenerative medicines developed from bioactive materials are gaining popularity for their targeted therapy delivery. Smart biomaterials are positioned to revolutionize healthcare, as they enable predictive, personalized care as the sector moves away from reactive approaches.

news.headline: Tuya Smart and DeepSeek are transforming AI into emotional companions, reshaping smart homes. March 21, 2025. TUYA on NYSE & HKEX.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, Tuya Smart, a leading AI cloud platform service provider, is revolutionizing the application of AI in smart homes. Listed on NYSE as TUYA and HKEX as 2391, the company is transforming technology into an emotionally intuitive ally within homes. Their innovative approach aims to enhance the home experience for individuals.

news.headline: Lennox (NYSE: LII) declares $1.15 quarterly dividend per share, payable on May 15, 2025, to stockholders of record by April 30, 2025.
news.summary: Dallas, TX - On March 21, 2025, the Board of Directors of Lennox (NYSE: LII) sanctioned a quarterly cash dividend of $1.15 for each share of common stock. This dividend will be disbursed on May 15, 2025, to shareholders recorded as of April 30, 2025. Lennox, a prominent player in energy-efficient solutions, continues to provide innovative services in the industry.

news.headline: Fed's Williams: I closely watch fiscal policy as it relates to economy and monetary policy.
Kanzhun's stock up 10% on solid earnings; net profit up 42.6% to 1.57 billion yuan, monthly active users grew 25.3% to 53 million.
news.summary: Kanzhun Ltd., the operator of China's largest online recruitment platform Boss Zhipin, reported significant growth in its financial performance. The company's annual revenue increased by 23.6% year-on-year to 7.36 billion yuan ($1.02 billion) and its net profit surged by 42.6% to 1.57 billion yuan. Kanzhun's monthly active users also grew by 25.3% to 53 million. The company ended the year with 14.7 billion yuan in cash and short-term investments. The overall positive performance reflects the potential opportunities in the recruitment industry as more people enter the workforce and companies show signs of ramping up recruitment activities. However, challenges remain, and the industry is expected to truly thrive once the broader economy picks up and companies increase their hiring efforts. For more details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Nike Inc exceeded earnings and revenue expectations in Q3 fiscal 2025, with revenue hitting $11.27 billion and earnings at 54 cents per share. Analysts upgraded their forecasts.
news.summary: Nike Inc (NYSE:NKE) exceeded expectations in its fiscal 2025 third-quarter financial report, with revenue reaching $11.27 billion, surpassing analyst projections of $11.01 billion. The company also reported earnings of 54 cents per share for the quarter, exceeding analyst estimates of 28 cents per share. Nike's President and CEO, Elliott Hill, expressed confidence in the company's progress in achieving its strategic goals, stating, "The progress we made against the ‘Win Now' strategic priorities we committed to 90 days ago reinforces my confidence that we are on the right path." This positive performance demonstrates the impact Nike has had in the market during this quarter. Detailed coverage is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Better Choice Company gets approval to acquire SRx Health Solutions & related proposals at special meeting on March 21, 2025.
news.summary: In Tampa, Florida on March 21, 2025, Better Choice Company, Inc. (NYSE American: BTTR), a leading pet health and wellness company, received shareholder approval for key proposals during a special meeting. The approved proposals were voted on during the March 21, 2025 meeting.

news.headline: Tata Communications launches Vayu Cloud fabric, revolutionizing cloud for intelligent enterprises.
news.summary: Tata Communications, a major global communications technology company and a key player in private Cloud services, unveiled Tata Communications Vayu on March 21, 2025 in Mumbai, India. The launch marks a significant step in the company's strategy, with Tata Communications' stock trading on the National Stock Exchange as TATACOMM and on the Bombay Stock Exchange as 500483. Tata Communications Vayu represents the latest innovation in Cloud technology, aimed at transforming Cloud fabric for improved services.

news.headline: NYC surgeon Jayme Locke awarded Excellence in Kidney Transplantation by NKF. Dr. Locke is VP of Medical Development at United Therapeutics.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, in New York, the National Kidney Foundation presented Dr. Jayme Locke with the award. Dr. Locke holds multiple roles including Vice President of Medical Development for Xenotransplantation at United Therapeutics, Adjunct Professor of Surgery at NYU Langone Health, and Member of the American Society of Transplant Surgeons.

news.headline: Cryptocurrency prices, including Bitcoin and Ethereum, dropped 2.4% due to 'triple witching', with BTC falling below $84,000 and ETH at $1,948.93.
news.summary: In recent market activity, the price of cryptocurrency has experienced a decrease along with other major coins due to anticipation of heightened market volatility. At this time, the value of has decreased by 2.4% from the previous day's value, falling below $84,000. Following suit, has also dropped by 2.4%, now being traded at $1,948.93 per coin based on data from CoinGecko. It is worth noting that the value of hasn't been this low since November.

news.headline: FedEx's Q3 earnings below expectations. Quarterly revenue $22.2B (+$0.5B YoY), EPS $4.51 (+$0.04 miss). Analysts lower forecasts.
news.summary: FedEx Corp. (NYSE: FDX) released its third-quarter results, reporting earnings of $4.51 per share, missing the analyst consensus of $4.54. Quarterly revenue stood at $22.2 billion, surpassing the estimated $21.89 billion and showing growth from $21.7 billion in the same period last year. CEO Raj Subramaniam acknowledged the challenging operational landscape, citing a successful navigation through obstacles like a condensed peak season and severe weather conditions. However, due to uncertainties regarding retirement plans, the company refrained from offering an outlook on fiscal 2025 earnings per share or effective tax rate. For further details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: TESCO introduces T3 Turbo Meter Tester, a quicker way to test meters in the field. Paired with 6330 Meter Site Analyzer for comprehensive diagnostics.
news.summary: The T3, in conjunction with the 6330 Meter Site Analyzer, offers a comprehensive solution for field testing, guaranteeing thorough meter and site diagnostics for various situations. Recently in Bristol, Pennsylvania on March 21, 2025, utilities encountered increasing difficulties in meter testing and site diagnostics.

news.headline: Micron beats Q2 expectations with revenue of $8.05B, up from $5.82B YoY, and earnings of $1.56 per share. Forecast for Q3: $8.8B. CEO pleased with data center revenue growth.
news.summary: Micron Technology Inc (NASDAQ:MU) posted impressive second-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations. The company reported second-quarter revenue reaching $8.05 billion, exceeding the estimated $7.89 billion, marking an increase from $5.82 billion year-over-year. Micron also revealed adjusted earnings of $1.56 per share for the quarter, higher than the anticipated $1.42 per share by analysts per Benzinga Pro. President and CEO Sanjay Mehrotra highlighted the company's fiscal Q2 EPS above guidance, with data center revenue tripling from the previous year. Micron anticipates third-quarter revenue of $8.8 billion, with a possible variance of $200 million.

Glenfarne Group names new directors for Texas LNG project-development in Port of Brownsville.
news.summary: In New York and Houston, Glenfarne Group, LLC ("Glenfarne"), a company specializing in energy and infrastructure assets, and Texas LNG Brownsville, LLC ("Texas LNG"), a liquefied natural gas export terminal with a capacity of four million tonnes per annum in Port of Brownsville, have revealed advancements in their senior leadership teams. These promotions, taking effect immediately, honor the executives for their significant roles in the organizations' key operations and development.

news.headline: Neonode Inc. (NASDAQ: NEON) reports 2024 results: $3.1 million revenue, a decrease of 18.8% from 2023.
news.summary: Neonode Inc. (NASDAQ: NEON) announced its financial performance for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. In this period, the company generated revenues of $3.1 million from continuing operations, marking an 18.8% decline compared to the previous year.

These expedited purchases often come with buyer incentives. news.headline: Ferrellgas Partners, L.P. reveals retirement of CFO.
news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global economy is projected to grow by approximately 4.5% in the next year, with emerging markets expected to lead the way with a growth rate of around 6%. However, developed economies are forecasted to see slower growth at approximately 3%. In terms of specific regions, Asia-Pacific is anticipated to experience the highest growth rate at around 7%, while Europe is projected to have a growth rate of approximately 4%. These projections highlight the ongoing shift in economic power towards emerging markets and the importance of diversifying investments across different regions.

news.headline: Navigate360 strengthens partnership with CRG & 911Cellular for emergency management solutions, rivaling CENTEGIX & Raptor.
news.summary: In Richfield, Ohio on March 21, 2025, Navigate360, a top provider of comprehensive school safety solutions, has unveiled an improved collaboration with Critical Response Group (CRG) and 911Cellular. This partnership is set to strengthen Navigate360's safety capabilities.

news.headline: Opus Genetics presents Phentolamine Ophthalmic Solution 0.75% study on dim light disturbances at world congress.
news.summary: Opus Genetics, Inc., a clinical-stage ophthalmic biotechnology company listed on Nasdaq under the symbol IRD, shared news today from Research Triangle Park, N.C., on March 21, 2025. The company is focused on developing gene therapies for inherited retinal diseases (IRDs) and other ophthalmic disorders. They revealed details of the upcoming presentation at the World Cornea Congress IX in Washington, D.C., scheduled for March 20-22, 2025. The presentation will discuss the LYNX-1 Phase 3 study of Phentolamine Ophthalmic Solution 0.75% designed for patients experiencing dim light disturbances. This study reflects the company's commitment to advancing innovative treatments in this field.

news.headline: US Trade Chief Greer set for China counterpart call next week.
BP sells 25% stake in TANAP pipeline to Apollo for $1 billion, aims for low-carbon collaboration.
news.summary: BP announced on Friday that Apollo Global Management-managed funds are set to purchase a 25% non-controlling stake in BP Pipelines (TANAP) Ltd – bp TANAP for approximately $1.0 billion. This subsidiary encompasses BP's 12% interest in TANAP, a 1,800 km pipeline facilitating the transportation of natural gas from Azerbaijan to Turkey. TANAP plays a vital role in the Southern Gas Corridor (SGC) pipeline system, channeling gas from the BP-operated Shah Deniz field in Azerbaijan to European markets, including Italy and Greece. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Clearmind Medicine's CMND-100 drug arrives in U.S., advancing FDA-approved trial for Alcohol Use Disorder affecting 28M adults.
news.summary: Vancouver-based company Clearmind Medicine Inc. (Nasdaq: CMND) and (FSE: CWY0) shared the arrival of their drug candidate, CMND-100, in the United States. The innovative psychedelic-derived therapeutic has completed manufacturing and the company is gearing up to start an FDA-approved Phase I/IIa clinical trial. This trial will focus on treating Alcohol Use Disorder (AUD), a condition that impacts more than 28 million American adults. Clearmind's aim is to address significant unmet medical needs through the development of this drug.

news.headline: Diana Shipping Inc. invests in joint venture for two 7,500 cbm semi-refrigerated LPG newbuildings, first delivery in Q1 2027.
news.summary: In Athens, Greece, on March 21, 2025, Diana Shipping Inc. revealed its plan to enter a strategic partnership and invest in Ecogas Holding AS, a joint venture, by acquiring an 80% equity stake. The partnership aims to construct two 7,500 cubic meter semi-refrigerated LPG newbuildings, with a provision to add two more vessels. The initial vessel is scheduled for delivery in Q1 of 2027, followed by the second vessel in Q4 of the same year. Diana Shipping Inc., listed on NYSE as DSX, focuses on owning and chartering dry bulk carriers globally.

news.headline: SEC’s decision to drop XRP case was anticipated since Trump’s election. Ripple CEO revealed the $1.3B lawsuit dismissal, stating markets had factored in this development. XRP token struggles to maintain $2.5 mark, dropping 6.3% post-announcement. Analysts cite lack of momentum due to anticipated lawsuit end and market sentiment. Technical analysis shows potential 75% XRP rally post-lawsuit end, with an ultimate target of $4.35 by June. Analysts predict favorable long-term market impact.
news.summary: The United States Securities and Exchange Commission announced the dismissal of its legal action against Ripple, ending four years of litigation over an alleged $1.3 billion unregistered securities offering. Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse stated that the market had already priced in this development since President Trump's election, dampening the impact on XRP's price. Despite an initial 11% relief rally, XRP fell over 6.3% and struggled to stay above the $2.5 mark. Analysts attributed this to investors anticipating the lawsuit's end and poor market sentiment. Some technical patterns suggest a potential 75% rally post-lawsuit end, with a $4.35 target by June. The SEC's decision could have a positive long-term effect on the market by fostering a more crypto-friendly environment.

news.headline: South Korea plans to block non-compliant crypto exchanges for operating without reporting to regulators. Authorities eye sanctions for services to Korean investors. Regulatory crackdown on exchanges violating laws; raids on Bithumb over embezzlement suspicions. Exchange KuCoin also under scrutiny.
news.summary: South Korean authorities are investigating crypto exchanges operating without complying with regulations. The Financial Intelligence Unit is considering sanctions against exchanges not reporting as virtual asset service providers (VASPs). The exchanges on the list allegedly provided marketing and customer support to Korean investors without following compliance. KuCoin was mentioned on the list. Exchanges must report to the FIU or face legal consequences. The FIU is reviewing measures to block access to the non-compliant exchanges. Prosecutors also raided Bithumb over embezzlement suspicions, and rumors surfaced about paid listings on Bithumb and Upbit. Upbit has demanded disclosure of project lists that paid brokerage fees.

Fed's Williams: It is key to think about longer term impact of government policy changes.
S.C. Department of Commerce honored with Townes Award by GSSM at 31st Annual Celebration.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, in Hartsville, South Carolina, the South Carolina Department of Commerce (S.C. Commerce) received the Townes Award from the South Carolina Governor's School for Science & Mathematics (GSSM). This prestigious award recognizes S.C. Commerce for its leadership and significant contributions. The ceremony took place on Wednesday evening, honoring the department for their exceptional work.

news.headline: Shell announces the final investment decision for Gato do Mato project in Brazil, expected to produce 120,000 barrels/day.
news.summary: Shell PLC's subsidiary, Shell Brasil Petróleo Ltda., has officially approved the Final Investment Decision (FID) for the Gato do Mato project situated in the pre-salt region of the Santos Basin off the coast of Brazil. The Gato do Mato Consortium, led by Shell holding a 50% stake, also includes Ecopetrol (30%), TotalEnergies (20%), and Pré-Sal Petróleo S.A. (PPSA), managing the production sharing contract (PSC). The initiative entails the installation of a floating production storage and offloading (FPSO) unit capable of producing up to 120,000 barrels of oil per day.

news.headline: Bitcoin falls to $76,600, down 25% from $110,000 high. "Perfect rejection" risks drop to $65K amid bearish sentiment and patterns.
news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) saw a 14% rebound from a four-month low near $76,600 on March 11 but is down 25% from its record high of $110,000, as part of a "bull market correction." Analysts predict further declines with "dark cloud cover" pattern indicating potential topping out. Failure to close within $90,000-$93,000 resistance zone signifies lack of buying conviction. Risk of $65,000 due to "perfect rejection" at $86,000-$88,000 resistance zone. Further drop to $77,000-$79,000 support zone and $65,000-$74,000 probable if support breaks. Correlation with S&P 500 and Nasdaq, both showing bear flag patterns, suggests potential sell-off towards $72,000. BTC's correlation with equities heightened amidst global trade war risks. Arthur Breitman warns of US recession as a major external risk for the crypto market. Investment decisions should be made after conducting thorough research.

news.headline: Fed's Williams: I am watching and collecting data about the impact of change in government policy.
Outcrop Silver launches $5M public offering of units, backed by Eric Sprott's investment.
news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that the global economy is facing a severe downturn, with the GDP dropping by 5% in the last quarter alone. Additionally, unemployment rates have skyrocketed to 15%, marking a significant increase from the previous year. The stock market has also been struggling, with major indices experiencing a decline of over 20% since the beginning of the year. Experts are predicting a slow recovery process, with the situation expected to worsen before showing any signs of improvement. Furthermore, governments around the world have been implementing various stimulus packages to mitigate the economic impact of the crisis. It is a challenging time for businesses and individuals alike as they navigate through these uncertain times.

news.headline: Fed's Williams: The Fed needs to be very focused on data right now.
Fed's Williams: Monetary policy is well positioned to achieve Fed goals.

Global battery housing market worth $8.2 billion in 2024, projected to reach $15.4 billion by 2034, growing at 7.2% CAGR. Industry driven by EV adoption, material innovation, sustainability. Challenges include supply chain disruptions, raw material costs, and regulatory pressures. Market trends towards lightweight materials, modular design, and high-performance battery technology. Toyota plans to introduce solid-state batteries in 2023.
news.summary: The global battery housing market is currently valued at $8.2 billion in 2024, driven by the increasing shift towards electric vehicles and renewable energy storage. It is projected to reach $15.4 billion by 2034, growing at a rate of 7.2% annually. Battery housings now play a crucial role in vehicle performance, structural integrity, and thermal management, adopting lightweight materials like aluminum and high-strength composites. Challenges include supply chain disruptions and raw material costs, but the market outlook remains positive. The growth is also attributed to the surge in EV adoption and battery technology advancements, such as solid-state batteries, leading to the need for innovative and protective battery enclosures. The industry is focusing on smart design, material innovation, and integrated safety solutions to meet the increasing demand for sustainable transportation and energy solutions.

news.headline: CEO of QOL Medical, LLC, Frederick "Derick" Cooper, donates $75,000 to Beacon Rescue for disaster relief efforts.
news.summary: In Vero Beach, Florida on March 21, 2025, Frederick "Derick" Cooper, the CEO of QOL Medical, a company that focuses on medications for rare gastrointestinal illnesses, has given a charitable contribution of $75,000 to Beacon Rescue. Beacon Rescue is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization specializing in Search and Rescue operations. The donation comes as a generous gesture from Cooper, known for his leadership at QOL Medical.

news.headline: Capriotti's celebrates National Cheesesteak Day with BOGO 50% off cheesesteaks, previews new Cheesesteak BLT launch.
news.summary: A popular sandwich chain, Capriotti's Sandwich Shop, known for its award-winning creations, is marking National Cheesesteak Day with a special promotion in Las Vegas. To celebrate the occasion on March 21, 2025, customers can enjoy a Buy One, Get One 50% Off deal on all items. Additionally, the chain is providing a sneak peek of their upcoming launch of the new Cheesesteak BLT. This limited-time offer is a great opportunity for fans of Capriotti's to indulge in their favorite sandwiches while exploring new flavors.

news.headline: Carnival Corporation & PLC reports record first quarter operating results with revenues of $5.8 billion, operating income of $543 million. Raised full-year 2025 guidance and advanced bookings are at historical highs. Various refinancing efforts reduced debt balance and interest expenses. Adjusted net income expected up over 30% from 2024. Achieving 2026 financial targets ahead of schedule. Potential capital expenditures and debt maturities also noted. Conference call scheduled for further details.
news.summary: Carnival Corporation & plc reported its financial results for Q1 2025, with record revenues of $5.8 billion, up over $400 million from the prior year. They achieved record net yields and operating income, with the latter almost doubling from the previous year. The company also refinanced $5.5 billion of debt during the quarter, leading to $145 million in annualized interest savings and reducing the debt balance by $0.5 billion. They expect adjusted net income for 2025 to increase by over 30% compared to 2024, exceeding December guidance by $185 million. The company is on track to achieve its 2026 financial targets a year in advance, reaching the highest levels in nearly two decades. Bookings for 2026 and beyond reached record levels. Carnival Cruise Line was voted the best ocean cruise line, with other highlights including partnerships and recognition for several brands. The company's 2025 outlook includes expectations of net yields approximately 4.7% higher than 2024, adjusted EBITDA of $6.7 billion, and adjusted net income up over 30% compared to 2024. They also proactively managed their debt profile by refinancing several debt obligations during the quarter, resulting in interest expense savings. Carnival Corporation & plc remains optimistic about the future despite global economic uncertainties, with their CFO mentioning a reduced average cash interest rate of 4.6%. Their debt maturities for the remainder of 2025 and full-year 2026 are $1.1 billion and $2.7 billion, respectively. Carnival Corporation & plc is the world's largest global cruise company, comprising various cruise lines. For more information, visit their official website.
